channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: English Clubs Struggle in Champions League First Legs, Managers Call for Patience
item.description: This week no be easy one for English clubs for the UEFA Champions League last-16 first legs as all six EPL teams no fit win. Liverpool, under Arne Slot, lost 1-0 away to Galatasaray, while Arsenal dra...

item.title: South Africa faces a crisis of identity and faith amid social and political challenges
item.description: The letter argues that South Africa is grappling less with a political crisis and more with a profound crisis of identity and faith. While the country has established a democratic framework with a con...

item.title: Guide to Cheltenham Gold Cup 2026 Betting Offers and Promotions
item.description: The 2026 Cheltenham Festival concludes with the prestigious Cheltenham Gold Cup, scheduled for 16:00 on Friday, boasting a prize fund of £625,000. This year, Gaelic Warrior is leading the market odds,...

item.title: Man Arrested for Allegedly Assaulting Woman under False Pretext of Fertility Treatment
item.description: In Balrampur village, a self-styled godman named Bhagwandin has been arrested for allegedly raping a 23-year-old woman by falsely claiming that he could help her conceive a son through rituals and tra...

item.title: Lacson urges revised affidavits from former Marines before Blue Ribbon hearing
item.description: Senate President Pro Tempore Panfilo 'Ping' Lacson clarified that the Blue Ribbon Committee remains open to investigating claims made by 18 alleged former Philippine Marines regarding a supposed cash ...

item.title: Edmonton Police Chief's Trip to Israel Sparks Community Criticism
item.description: The Chief of the Edmonton Police Service, Warren Driechel, is facing significant backlash from Edmonton's Muslim-Canadian community and Islamic mosques following a February trip to Israel as part of a...

item.title: Permanent Secretary Explains Purpose of 'Talk to the Minister' Digital Platform for Public Health Feedback
item.description: The Permanent Secretary in the Ministry of Health, Dr Seif Shekalaghe, has explained that the digital platform known as 'Talk to the Minister' was started by the Minister of Health, Honourable Mohamed...

item.title: Guidelines for Students on Ethical and Effective Use of AI in Education
item.description: As students progress through secondary school and university, they face a significant shift with AI becoming more prevalent in education. While there is concern about students misusing AI, most are ea...

item.title: Tanzania urges strong plans to maximize benefits from GC8 health funding

item.title: Government launches 50bn/- fund to empower young creatives in Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzanian government, through the Ministry of Information, Culture, Arts and Sports, has announced a new initiative to support young people in the creative sector with a fund worth 50 billion Tanz...

item.title: CAPF and local police conduct night-time vehicle inspections ahead of Assembly elections in Nagapattinam
item.description: In preparation for the 2026 Assembly elections, Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) personnel, in collaboration with the local police, have been conducting night-time vehicle checks across various loca...

item.title: Traffic Disruptions in Islamabad and Karachi Due to Al-Quds Day Processions
item.description: Today, several main roads in Karachi and Islamabad were closed due to security measures for Al-Quds Day rallies. Al-Quds Day is observed internationally on the last Friday of Ramazan to show solidarit...

item.title: Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen to Step Down After Leading Company for 18 Years
item.description: Shantanu Narayen, the CEO of Adobe since 2007, has announced that he will step down once a successor is appointed, concluding an 18-year tenure during which he transformed Adobe from a traditional sof...

item.title: Proposed Philippine Council on AI: Structure, Duties, and Bills
item.description: The Philippine government aims to establish the Philippine Council on Artificial Intelligence (PCAI) through multiple bills filed in Congress starting late 2025, including Senate Bill 852 and House Bi...

item.title: Converting Africa’s mineral resources into sustainable industry and employment
item.description: Africa possesses immense mineral wealth, valued at US$29.5 trillion in current mine sites and US$8.6 trillion still undeveloped, according to the Africa Finance Corporation. However, much of this valu...

item.title: Hawthorn Dominates Essendon in AFL Clash, Young Bombers Gain Experience
item.description: In a commanding display, Hawthorn outplayed Essendon in their AFL clash, leaving the Bombers struggling to keep up. The Hollywood Hawks showed excellent teamwork and goal-scoring power, with standout ...

item.title: Iran asserts its right to participate in the World Cup despite U.S. safety concerns
item.description: Iran has declared that it cannot be excluded from the 2026 FIFA World Cup, responding to comments from former U.S. President Donald Trump. Trump had warned that Iranian players’ safety could be at ris...

item.title: Early Benchmarks Show Apple's MacBook Neo Outperforming Top x86 CPUs in Single-Core Tests
item.description: Benchmark results from Notebookcheck reveal that the new Apple MacBook Neo, powered by the A18 Pro chip, delivers record-breaking single-core performance that surpasses all current x86 processors from...

item.title: Controller of Budget Corrects Misattributed Sh44.5 Million Expenditure
item.description: The Controller of Budget, Margaret Nyakang'o, has clarified reports claiming that the Second Lady, Joyce Kithure, spent Sh44.5 million in the first half of the 2025/26 financial year. According to OCo...

item.title: DOJ Raises Evidence Standard in Preliminary Investigations: What You Need to Know
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) recently increased the amount of evidence prosecutors need to file a case in court, a move upheld by the Supreme Court (SC). Under Department Circular No. 15, series of...
